items.title: Bonus Hollander POTD Purvis Short. Jackson St F drafted 5th by GS in 78. Took a few yrs but was scoring machine, raining jumpers from all over. Peaked at 28 ppg in 84-85. Never made all-star team cuz GS was usually bad. Finished up w/HOU + NJ (87-90). Now works for NBPA.

items.title: This will use up the remainder of the Sixers' MLE, pushing the Hukporti signing into the bi-annual exception.
items.author: Derek Bodner
items.body_excerpt:
This will use up the remainder of the Sixers' MLE, pushing the Hukporti signing into the bi-annual exception.

The Sixers now have $2.062m remaining on the bi-annual, and a $4.2 million trade exception, at their disposal if they wish.

They are $4.3 million below the hard cap.

items.title: Love that Anfernee Simons contract for the Sixers. What a killer get to give them a third guard.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Love that Anfernee Simons contract for the Sixers. What a killer get to give them a third guard.

Looks like he'll get the rest of the NTMLE (splitting it with Dean Wade), which means that Ariel Hukporti's contract will probably come out of the Bi-Annual Exception.

items.body_excerpt: Jeff Teague, DJ Wells, and B Hen react to Stephen A. Smith of ESPN going on his podcast and GOING OFF on the Los Angeles Lakers only signing white players. Their lineup is built around Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves, and Walker Kessler, and Stephen A. talks about how the Lakers…

items.title: Sixers will use the remainder of the non-tax mid-level here and fit Ariel Hukporti into the bi-annual exception. Simons provides a major scoring jolt for the second unit. Badly needed.
items.author: Adam Aaronson
items.body_excerpt: Sixers will use the remainder of the non-tax mid-level here and fit Ariel Hukporti into the bi-annual exception. Simons provides a major scoring jolt for the second unit. Badly needed.

items.title: Just in: Free agent guard Anfernee Simons has agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal with the Philadelphia 76ers, with a player option in second season, sources tell ESPN. Simons chose the 76ers over other suitors believing his fit is perfect with the revamped 76ers roster.
items.author: Shams Charania Tweets
items.body_excerpt: Just in: Free agent guard Anfernee Simons has agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal with the Philadelphia 76ers, with a player option in second season, sources tell ESPN. Simons chose the 76ers over other suitors believing his fit is perfect with the revamped 76ers roster.
